title: agathis orientalis
intro: Agathis orientalis, also known as the Malabar kino tree or Indian kino tree, is a large evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The tree's bark is rich in tannins and is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Agathis orientalis have been found to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and regulating blood sugar levels. The bark is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of traditional medicines and supplements.
